teachingPoint: IR-T637-1 — T637 TemplateSchemaValidator MUST enforce three-gate JSON Schema validation. JSON Schema structure validation at ORDER 1 — template.schema must be valid JSON Schema (draft-07, draft-2020-12). Required field enforcement at ORDER 2 — required fields in template.requiredFields must exist in schema.required array. Type compatibility at ORDER 3 — field types must match ALLOWED_FIELD_TYPES compile-time constant. SchemaInvalid emitted on any validation gate failure. storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 4 BEFORE enqueue. DNA-8.
positiveExample: T637: JSON Schema validation at ORDER 1 → required field enforcement at ORDER 2 → type compatibility at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 4 → enqueue(TemplateValidated) at ORDER 5. SchemaInvalid emitted on any validation failure with phase indicator.
negativeExample: T637 skips JSON Schema validation — invalid schemas pass through. Or T637 checks type compatibility before required fields — missing required fields trigger type errors instead of required field errors. Or T637 enqueues before audit — outbox pattern violated.
discriminatingConstraint: JSON Schema validation ORDER 1, required field enforcement ORDER 2, type compatibility ORDER 3, audit before enqueue. Missing any gate or wrong order = BUILD_FAILURE. CF-23-1.
keywords: JSON Schema validation required field enforcement type compatibility ALLOWED_FIELD_TYPES SchemaInvalid TemplateValidated T637 TemplateSchemaValidator

teachingPoint: IR-T638-1 — T638 TemplateVersionPublisher MUST enforce OCC DRAFT→PUBLISHED with version immutability. OCC updateDocument(status:PUBLISHED, expectedVersion) at ORDER 1 prevents double-publication race. Version immutability at ORDER 2 — once PUBLISHED, template.schema cannot be modified; VersionImmutableRejected emitted on published template modification attempt. Schema evolution check at ORDER 3 — new schema must be forward-compatible superset of previous published versions (additive only, no field removals). storeDocument(publication audit) at ORDER 4 BEFORE VersionPublished enqueue. DNA-8.
positiveExample: T638: OCC updateDocument(status:PUBLISHED, version:+1) at ORDER 1 → version immutability guard at ORDER 2 → schema evolution check at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 4 → enqueue(VersionPublished) at ORDER 5. TemplatePublicationConflict on OCC failure. VersionImmutableRejected on published modification attempt.
negativeExample: T638 publishes without OCC — double-publication race creates two PUBLISHED versions. Or T638 checks immutability after evolution — partial write to published template. Or T638 allows field removal in schema — backward incompatibility. Or T638 enqueues before audit — outbox pattern violated.
discriminatingConstraint: OCC updateDocument ORDER 1, version immutability ORDER 2, schema evolution check ORDER 3, audit before enqueue. Non-OCC publish, immutability after write, plain storeDocument, or enqueue before audit = BUILD_FAILURE. CF-23-2.
keywords: OCC DRAFT PUBLISHED immutability schema evolution forward-compatible TemplatePublicationConflict VersionPublished VersionImmutableRejected T638 TemplateVersionPublisher

teachingPoint: IR-T639-1 — T639 TemplateInstantiationEngine MUST use atomic SETNX lock before variable binding. SETNX instantiation lock at ORDER 1 with key template-instantiate-lock:{templateId}:{contextId} prevents concurrent instantiations. Variable binding at ORDER 2 — resolve ${variable} patterns using provided bindings map. Default merge at ORDER 3 — populate fields with template-defined defaults after binding. storeDocument(form instance) at ORDER 4 BEFORE enqueue. DNA-8. redis.del(lockKey) MUST be called in finally block to prevent hung locks.
positiveExample: T639: SETNX(template-instantiate-lock:{templateId}:{contextId}) at ORDER 1 → variable binding at ORDER 2 → default merge at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(form instance) at ORDER 4 → enqueue(FormInstantiated) at ORDER 5. Finally: redis.del(lockKey). InstantiationAlreadyInProgress on lock held.
negativeExample: T639 binds variables before SETNX — concurrent instantiations overwrite each other. Or T639 skips redis.del in finally — hung lock blocks all future instantiations permanently. Or T639 merges defaults before binding — variables override defaults instead of binding values. Or T639 enqueues before store — outbox pattern violated.
discriminatingConstraint: SETNX at ORDER 1 before binding, binding at ORDER 2 before defaults, store before enqueue, redis.del in finally. Missing SETNX, binding before lock, missing redis.del, or enqueue before store = BUILD_FAILURE. CF-23-3.
keywords: SETNX template-instantiate-lock variable binding default merge redis.del lock release FormInstantiated InstantiationAlreadyInProgress T639 TemplateInstantiationEngine

teachingPoint: IR-T640-1 — T640 TemplateUsageAnalytics MUST maintain append-only metrics with PII exclusion. Append-only metrics storeDocument at ORDER 1 — never updateDocument on usage records. PII_EXCLUDED_FIELDS = [formData, userInput, values, responseData] compile-time constant at ORDER 2 — user input values NOT stored; only metadata recorded. Popularity scoring at ORDER 3 — rank templates by (instantiation_count + submission_count) / template_age_days. All metrics remain PRIVATE knowledgeScope per tenant. UsageMetricsRecorded emitted to enable downstream analytics.
positiveExample: T640: Append-only metrics storeDocument at ORDER 1 → PII exclusion filters user values at ORDER 2 → popularity scoring at ORDER 3 → enqueue(UsageMetricsRecorded) at ORDER 4. Every instantiation/submission creates new metrics record. Metrics PRIVATE per tenant. No cross-tenant visibility.
negativeExample: T640 updates metrics records — non-repudiation audit trail destroyed. Or T640 stores user input values — PII leakage. Or T640 makes metrics visible across tenants — cross-tenant analytics leakage. Or T640 does not compute popularity score — downstream analytics cannot rank templates by usage.
discriminatingConstraint: Append-only: storeDocument only. PII exclusion: user input values excluded. Popularity computed. Metrics PRIVATE per tenant. updateDocument on metrics, user values in storage, or cross-tenant visibility = BUILD_FAILURE. CF-23-4.
keywords: append-only metrics PII exclusion formData userInput popularity scoring instantiation submission UsageMetricsRecorded T640 TemplateUsageAnalytics

teachingPoint: SCHEMA-VALIDATION-BEFORE-PUBLICATION-001 — Templates must pass validation (T637) before publication (T638) is attempted. This prevents invalid schemas from being published and serves as a pre-flight check. Validation failure blocks publication; no recovery path exists except to fix and re-validate.
positiveExample: T637 validates schema → JSON Schema valid, required fields enforced, types match → TemplateValidated emitted. T638 then publishes with confidence that schema is valid.
negativeExample: T638 publishes without T637 validation — invalid schemas reach PUBLISHED state. Or T637 validates but T638 publishes without checking validation result — validation is bypassed.
discriminatingConstraint: T637 validation is prerequisite for T638 publication. Validation failure at any ORDER blocks pipeline. No publication without validation. Missing validation = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: schema validation publication prerequisite T637 T638 TemplateValidated VersionPublished

teachingPoint: VARIABLE-BINDING-LOCK-ORDERING-001 — Template instantiation must acquire lock BEFORE attempting variable binding. This ensures that concurrent instantiation attempts are serialized and variable resolution happens only once per template:context pair. Lock must be released in finally block to prevent hung states.
positiveExample: T639: Acquire SETNX lock → bind variables → merge defaults → store instance. Lock prevents concurrent binding. redis.del in finally releases lock on any outcome.
negativeExample: T639 binds variables before acquiring lock — two concurrent instantiations both pass binding stage. Or T639 acquires lock but doesn't redis.del in finally — hung lock permanently blocks future instantiations.
discriminatingConstraint: Lock ORDER 1 before binding. redis.del in finally block. Missing lock or redis.del = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: SETNX lock variable binding redis.del finally ordering T639
